task_completion.visual_evidence: The Juris-M application window is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: The target case is located and selected
task_completion.visual_evidence: The entry for 'Brown v. Board of Education' is visible and highlighted/selected in the Juris-M library items list.

task_completion.description: A note editor is open for the target case
task_completion.visual_evidence: The note pane (usually on the right side) or a standalone note window is open and clearly associated with the 'Brown v. Board of Education' entry.

task_completion.description: Text is entered into the note
task_completion.visual_evidence: There is typed text visible inside the Juris-M note editor.

task_completion.description: The note describes the legal significance of the case
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text in the note discusses the legal impact of Brown v. Board of Education (e.g., mentioning desegregation, unconstitutionality of 'separate but equal', or the 14th Amendment).

integrity.description: Agent used the Juris-M GUI rather than terminal commands
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows showing SQLite commands directly modifying the jurism.sqlite database.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Juris-M application
integrity.visual_evidence: The note is being written inside the Juris-M interface, not in a generic text editor like gedit, nano, or a web browser.

integrity.description: Agent wrote an actual description rather than copying the prompt instructions
integrity.visual_evidence: The note contains historical/legal facts about the case, not just the literal phrase 'describing the legal significance of the case'.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target case: 'Brown v. Board of Education'. The note must contain a description of the legal significance of the case (e.g., ruling that racial segregation in public schools is unconstitutional, overturning Plessy v. Ferguson).
